question.text: What is 'Rho' in the context of option Greeks?

answer.text: Rho measures the sensitivity of option premium to changes in the risk-free interest rate — call options have positive Rho (higher rates increase call value) and put options have negative Rho (higher rates decrease put value)
Rho measures the sensitivity of option premium to changes in the underlying stock price
Rho is the same as delta but for put options only
Rho has no practical significance and is never used in trading

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Rho = ΔPremium / ΔInterest rate. Higher interest rates increase the cost of carrying the underlying, making calls more valuable (easier to buy later) and puts less valuable. Rho impact is larger for longer-dated options and less significant for short-dated options. **The Trap:** Price sensitivity is delta, not rho. Rho applies to both calls and puts. While Rho has less impact than delta/gamma/theta/vega for short-dated options, it matters for LEAPS and longer-term options. **Cross-Exam Context:** For currency options (I Ch4), Rho is particularly important because TWO interest rates affect pricing. For IR options (IV Ch4), the interest rate IS the underlying, making rate sensitivity dominant. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Rho matters most when rates are changing rapidly — during RBI rate hiking cycles, longer-dated option premiums adjust for Rho even before the underlying moves.
